summary:
HONORING MILITARY SACRIFICE: Brandon expresses profound gratitude to servicemen and women for their sacrifices to protect American freedom. Brandon acknowledges the deep physical and emotional trauma many veterans endure, specifically mentioning PTSD, nightmares, and the scars of conflicts like the Vietnam War, and prays for their peace of mind.

VISION OF GENERATIONAL WEALTH: Brandon emphasizes the importance of believing in grand visions, drawing inspiration from Oral Roberts and his mandate to make no little plans. Brandon asserts that poverty is a curse, referencing struggles with basic sustenance and the conditions in impoverished nations, and argues that believers should cultivate an inward image of prosperity. Brandon also mentions standing on divine revelations regarding cryptocurrencies, gold, and silver to build generational wealth to help others.

REJECTING SATANIC TEMPTATIONS: Brandon recounts a personal visitation from Satan, who offered immense worldly wealth, including jets and luxury cars, in exchange for serving the kingdom of darkness. Brandon completely rejected this offer, declaring that the devil has nothing good to offer and that the true desire of his heart is the presence and anointing of God.

THE COST OF THE ANOINTING: Brandon explains that walking in divine glory requires enduring immense pressure and a refiner's fire, likening the process to an olive oil press. Brandon expresses a desperate hunger for this anointing, primarily motivated by a desire to see supernatural healings for special needs children, including his own daughter Megan who has autism, rather than for self-glorification.

ESTABLISHING STRICT BOUNDARIES: Brandon addresses the fanaticism and lack of boundaries within the Christian community, severely warning individuals who stalk his postal box or attempt to lay hands on his daughter. Brandon firmly declares that no one is allowed to touch his family or pray over them without explicit consent, prioritizing safety and spiritual discernment over intrusive religious behavior.

INTIMACY WITH THE DIVINE: Brandon voices a deep frustration with church politics, fake ministries, and celebrity pastor culture, choosing instead to prioritize a raw, vulnerable relationship with Jesus. Brandon highlights historical figures of healing revivals like Smith Wigglesworth, John G. Lake, and Kathryn Kuhlman as examples of individuals who gained their impartation directly from sitting at the feet of God.

COMMUNION AND PROPHETIC HEALING: Brandon partakes in communion to claim physical healing and emphasizes that God does not tempt people to sin or give them sickness. Brandon concludes by prophetically commanding the restoration of marriages, the return of wayward children, and the salvation of unsaved loved ones, while declaring specific miraculous healings for various ailments including cancers, diabetes, and severely painful hormonal or menstrual issues affecting women.
rawData: [May 28, 2025]-[Pray with me today - when you get real with God - God will get real with you]-[https://youtu.be/lyStwJiJKBc]-[Hey, good morning family. Praise the Lord. Thank you for joining us today. Well, we want to take the time right now just to thank all our servicemen and women. I know a lot of you don't get enough appreciation. I feel in my heart there's a lot of you that are still going through the trials of war, from your body being mangled up to all kinds of things. You know, there's men that have lost their wives, there's women that have lost their husbands, and fathers and mothers that have lost their sons and daughters. And I want to just take the time to say thank you. Thank you. Thank you for sacrificing your life to make America great. Because of people like you, we're free. Because of people like you that have fought in those trenches, and went and got drafted in Vietnam, and all the terrible things of war, the things that you have seen, we pray peace over you today. We pray peace over your mind today. We pray peace that passes all understanding over you. I know there's a lot of you; you email me and you tell me, "I'm battling with PTSD, and I have nightmares every night." And my goodness, I pray peace over you. We know your sacrifice was great, and many people will never understand that sacrifice because they've not walked it. And so we just want to take the time to say thank you. Thank you for our country. Thank you for fighting for us. Because of you, we're not speaking another language. We're Americans and we just want to say thank you. God bless you. So, we're going to go over some of the things that we spoke about last time in the last video. You know, folks, I believe in God for big. That is my motto. It's what I teach my kids. That's what I teach. That's what my wife and I—we don't want to think small. You know, it's like Oral Roberts said, "Make no little plans here." It's all over his campus. "Make no little plans here. Think big. Think big." So when you drive around, it's like Oral Roberts University. You can go there so many times. I go out there all the time. Drive by every day almost and look at the campus because it inspires me to think and believe big—that that was somebody's faith, you know. And so when I talk about driving around cars, I really don't have a desire to have a fleet load of cars, but I think that's the best the world has to offer: Cadillacs, Range Rovers. That's what the Hollywood movie stars drive, you know. And, you know, we don't have a Rolls-Royce dealership or Ferrari. I drive around in those to inspire yourself to think bigger. When you're raised in poverty, when you're raised in what I was raised in, you don't have an inward image of wealth to think bigger for God to do bigger because you're so used to thinking small. You know, grandma was poor, her grandma was poor, everybody's been poor, and you'll be poor, too. No, I'm redeemed from the curse. And I believe poverty is a curse, regardless. I've never seen anybody that's not able to pay their bills, struggling and eating Spam and doing things. Well, people say, "I like Spam." Well, I don't. I don't. I personally don't. That's my opinion. So you know, you have people that are raised on certain things—Ramen noodles and things that you go, "Well, that's all we had because that's what we could afford." Living on food stamps, living on these things. That's a curse. True poverty is a curse. It's not a blessing. And people, you live in some of these remote areas in Haiti and Africa and in Asia or whatever, and you're living in a tin hut or whatever—that's not a blessing. I look at what everybody goes to work for Monday through Friday. The reason I say it, and I'm so strong about it, is because we all go to work every day to try to make the dream, to get enough money to put your kids through college, to pay your bills on time so they don't repo your car. I've been there, and I know that those times were sorrowful. That was hard. And believing God to get extra, selling on eBay and Poshmark and doing these things that we've done, stepping out by faith and finding things. There's even another level of, I see, wealth. And the Bible says, "Beloved, I wish above all things that you prosper and be in health, even as your soul prospers." There's so much in the Bible about walking in abundance. In abundance to the full, till it overflows. Not living barely getting by and broke. And yeah, people got you burned out because of the prosperity preaching and all that stuff, but I believe a lot of them started out good, trying to help out the body of Christ to break off the yoke of bondage, because I'm telling you, there's a lot of bondage with poverty. And I'm not saying that there's no bondage with prosperity, because there are a lot of people that lose themselves in it, and it is what it is. But I think there's a happy medium that you can have blessing as long as the blessing doesn't have you. So, that's what I'm talking about: getting that inward image of the blessing and walking in an abundance so you can help those around you. Help those around you. I've seen people—man, I wish, you know, you drive by and you see somebody that they don't have nothing, you're looking at them going, "Man, I wish I had a little extra to help them out." But there's so much blockage in the mind when it comes to the devil trying to tell you you can't have nothing. He doesn't wrestle with you trying to bless you. You know what happened with me? Let me tell you. I'll be real with you right now. Satan himself came to my house, and I used the visions that I have—he showed me all the wealth. He said, "If you bow your knee to me, if you bow your knee to me, I will give you all this wealth." I saw jets, I saw houses, I saw Ferraris, I saw all this. He said, "If you serve me and use the gift that you have for seeing for the kingdom of darkness, I will give you everything that your heart's desire." He showed it all to me. I saw his palace, and it was black onyx, and blood was flowing from his throne, and it was just similar to what happened, I believe, to what happened to Jesus: "If you serve me, I'll give you all this. I'll give you the kingdoms of the earth." To make a long story short, because I don't like talking about the darkness, there's nothing the devil has to offer in all that that I wanted. I want the anointing. I want the presence of the Father. And in Him, there's peace. There's joy. There's all those things. The devil has nothing good to offer. I've had, what, three visitations like that. The last one was to challenge the Word. And he spoke to me, and I said, "Are you coming to offer the kingdoms again?" And he said, "No. Once you reject me once, I never come and offer you anything again. I'm coming to challenge the Word." You know, like I said, I don't talk about the darkness. But I want you to know that even the devil tries to entice people with wealth. Why? Because it's what the world wants. We wake up every day, Monday through Friday, like I said. But how much can you be a believing believer and have it, and show the world that you can have Jesus and the blessing? You can have Jesus and the blessing. So, that's not my message for today, but I feel like a lot of people are hung up on poverty, and I refuse to take that vow. I refuse to take it for my kids. I want generational wealth. I'm believing for what He showed me about the cryptocurrencies. I'm believing for what He showed me about gold and silver, and what He showed me about these things. And I'm standing on what the Lord has showed me. I stand on that. Now, everything the Lord does, it's like I told Dino. We were talking about the anointing and the increase of the anointing. It seems to go with the price of the anointing and the price of walking in the glory. It seems to go through—you seem to go through the winepress where you feel the pressure of things. The character it takes to sustain it costs you something. You can't get that in the world. You can't walk in that kind of glory from the world. The devil can't offer that. What that is, is it's coming straight out of the throne room. And it was all bought and paid for by the blood of Jesus, folks. But I'm telling you, there is a price to pay for the anointing. There is a price to pay for walking in the glory. It comes through pressure. It comes through the refiner's fire. And most people don't want to hear that. But I've studied and I've looked at every single person that has walked in a great anointing, and they've all had a refiner's fire. They've all had pressure on them to the point where I thought, 'Does it look like they could make it out of it?' where your head is pulled underneath that water and you're grasping for air, and all you want and all you think about is just to get your next breath. It comes out of that pressure. How bad do you want change? How bad do you want the glory? How bad do you want the anointing on your life? It comes through something: the press, an oil press. Like whenever you see those olive oil presses and they're pressing that oil, and the more that oil presses down on there and clamps down on there, it presses all that good oil out of those olives. That's the way it is with you. Staying on the potter's wheel, staying on that wheel, dying to self. He's the potter, we're the clay. We have this treasure in earthen vessels, that the excellency of the power will be known of him and not of us. I'm so desperate, and I thought, 'Why do I want so badly the anointing? Why do I want to see that? Is it for self-glory?' No. My heart, when I see children with special needs like my Megan, who is overcoming—she's getting better and better every day. When I see that, you feel helpless without it. There's nothing you can do in the natural to fix it other than the anointing and a prayer. So the more I see it, it comes out of my spirit. It comes out of my spirit to see children of Down's and to know that there's more, that God is a God that is able to heal, that he is a rewarder of those that diligently seek him. It's like when I, one person, sowed a seed, and I sowed it by faith, but it was a sacrificial seed for Diana and me. We did it recently. And they said, 'Well, we're believing for a hundredfold return.' And I said, 'But what I want is not monetary.' Truly, I try to stretch myself to believe for more because I know what it takes to have a staff, to hire somebody to help us. And that's going to take a lot of money to go and say, 'Well, I'm going to relieve Diana of all this pressure on Diana to answer all these emails and all this stuff all the time so she can focus on other things, to hire an employee.' Well, that's going to take money. Well, that stretches me. So I have to believe more, to believe God for somebody else's salary because they're dependent on you for their check. But the whole part of what I'm after is not money. I'm after God, and I want him. And in him, I want the anointing so bad, and I go, 'Lord, there's a balance of everything.' But I'm like, 'Man, don't you all understand what I'm saying?' I say, 'You just want to see the dead raised? You just want to see blinded eyes open. You want to see ears pop open. You want to see people get out of wheelchairs.' But you say, 'Well, Brandon, I've never seen that before.' But I know that it exists. There's a level of the anointing that I know exists. I've experienced it, but to maintain it and keep it and not get into the flesh... Like certain preachers that I've seen on platforms, they had a great anointing, but it seems like they've lost it now. It's not as strong, definitely. And I've seen a lot of them, and you go, 'Where's that fire? What happened? You had it in the '90s. You had it in the early 2000s, but then it just kind of died.' I don't want to be a flame that shrivels up, that goes away, that gets put out. I want to live long and finish strong. But I've told the Lord many times, if I'm not able to handle this, if I'm not able to preach the gospel to the nations and I'm going to stumble and fall like some of these people through trials—their moral failures or whatever. And you see what happened even in our old church. So many people were hurt. So many people turned away from the Lord. When you see a moral failure, when you see these things, if I'm not able to handle it, and I don't want to ever be arrogant and say that I am able to... 'Oh, that would never happen to me,' because you see some of the greatest fall. I said, 'Lord, I'd rather not have it at all than cause a million people to fall.' That's what I'm trying to tell y'all, because of something that I don't see in myself. So I examine myself every day like that, because I see people on huge platforms that get really big, get a lot of money, and then they're ruined by it. I can name a bunch of them right now. But I don't judge them. I just don't want to be accountable for hurting people like that. Does that make sense? It's important to me to always be transparent, always be a person that... we all have stuff we're going through, folks. We're always... but I'm just me. I don't want to ever be exalted like you look at a celebrity. I'm just me. Now, I know there are certain lines when you show up at my PO box and you camp outside—like I'm mentioning somebody right now—and you demand to want to pray for my little girl and you say that God sent you to pray for her. And then you sit out there and you creep out the postal workers so much that they said if you show up again, they're calling the cops because you think that God's called you. Now, folks, I don't want to be... there's some weirdness out here in the body of Christ. There needs to be some stability. And I feel like it's not taught. You just don't go trying to lay hands on people's kids thinking because you feel like God's called you to. God didn't tell me that. And I don't know why I get off on that, but it is something that still bothers me, that there are people that are just consistently coming to my church trying to touch my kid because you feel you have an anointing on you to pray for my Megan. You'll never touch my child. Never. That's my child. And if God calls me to have somebody pray for my child, I'll take my child to that person. But you're not called to come laying hands on people unless God... unless I feel peace about it. And that's happened several times now. And it makes me, quite frankly, I get irate when I get in the car. I told... my son knows, everybody: do not touch my children. Do not touch me. That's why I said the Bible says do not lay hands on a man suddenly. And I do not, after encounters that I've had, I do not allow people to pray for me unless the Holy Spirit tells me to let them. I just don't. I would advise you to be wise about it too. You are not touching me. You're not touching my wife. You are not touching my children. So, it is what it is. But I felt like I need to say that. So, don't come to my PO box, try to knock on my house, find out where I live so you think you can lay hands on me and touch me or I'm going to touch you. Okay. God bless you. So, you know, I feel like it's important I say these things. There's an importance of the anointing and being hungry and impartation by the Spirit of God. But I don't need people laying hands on me for impartation. I need Jesus. I need Jesus in these last days, finding where it comes through prayer. Obviously, it comes through prayer, but it comes through sitting at his feet. Moses had an experience with a burning bush, and the Father was in the bush. I could go on. You can go on. David had this down, you know. Come on. There's so much impartation that didn't come from laying on hands, other than David had an impartation from the prophet. But I look and say, 'God, where are you? I just need you.' The Smith Wigglesworths, the John G. Lakes, the Kathryn Kuhlman's all had impartation from him sitting at his feet. I'm just desperate. I'm just desperate for an end-time move. I'm desperate for what we've not seen before. I know what's coming: the visions that I've had about the outpouring, the visions that I've had about the last outpouring of the healing revival and the demonstrations, the manifestations. And I thought, 'What is the domino to tip it?' Jesus did nothing without his Father. Nothing. Everything Jesus did was sitting at the Father's, going up in the mountains praying. Apart from him, he said, 'I do nothing.' So if Jesus did that, then what? That's what I'll do. Mary put—I've always said this—Mary put a demand on the anointing that was on Jesus's life at the marriage feast whenever he turned the water into wine. I know, putting a demand on it saying, 'Father, I thank you when you lay hands on someone that you're the healer. You're the deliverer.' I get that. But it's like the woman with the issue of blood. She reached out and touched the hem of the garment, and she put a demand on that anointing and she said, 'If I just touch the hem of his garment, I can be made whole.' Her faith made her whole. But she reached out in faith, and she grabbed onto something, and she pulled. She pulled. You pull in your spirit. You pull. It's like, 'I'm desperate. I'm desperate.' And in that desperation, he says, 'Those that seek me will find me. Those that knock, the door will be opened unto them.' So in desperation, I'm just seeking him. I'm pulling on that. 'Father, I need a touch. Father, I'm desperate. Father, I'm thirsty. Father, I need more of you. I need more revelation of you.' You say, 'I'm wall-to-wall Holy Ghost.' Then I need to give you more of me. Something's off because I want to be at the spout where the glory comes out. I want to be where he's at. I want that fire on me so strong that my shadow passes by and people are healed. Waking up every day with expectation for manifestation of the glory on your life. Not going and saying, 'Well, it's just another average day,' but when people see you, they see Jesus. Do you hear what I'm saying, folks? There is a point of that glory that I'm talking about where all this church politics—I'm so done with it. 'Are you on my YouTube channel? Are you going to be on collabs?' All this stuff. I just want Jesus. 'Well, you didn't come to my conference and...' I just want Jesus. And who said we're going to feel a presence all the time? But that's what I'm after. I want him. I want him. I'm so desperate for him. I want a fresh drink every day. And I do these videos, and I try to be as vulnerable as I can to teach you all how I've done this through my life. It's very awkward. People have no idea—I mean, now it's grown to like 60,000 every day. Every time I do this, 60,000 people—it's a lot, and everybody has a critic about it, and it's just me being me. All I can do is just be me. I don't know how else to do it other than to try to teach you the intimacy of my relationship with him. And it's so intimate, and it's so personal to me. And it's like having so many people criticize it and you go, 'But it's pure to me.' All I know is that I love him and I seek him with all my heart because he's my friend, and in my desperation, he visits. All this other crap doesn't matter to me. It truly doesn't. Popularity, whether you show up or not, I just do it because he asked me to. If he tells me to stop tomorrow, I will. Trust me, it's more convenient for me to stop. But I do it. I do it because he said, 'I want you to do it for me.' And I said, 'I'll do it for you. Help me.' And it's not easy for my flesh because I'm a very reclusive person. I try to be real with people on social media, but I don't like them knowing my business. I don't like them in my circle, my space. In fact, I truly never even talked to my family. I've been that way my whole life. Really, a person who kind of goes in his room and just does his own thing, but I have intimacy with the Father and I just want to talk to him. Do you want a God that you can talk to? I'm not religious. It's a relationship with me. But I expect him to show up, just like I expect my wife to talk back to me if I'm talking to her in the car, and if she doesn't, I'll say, 'You're being rude,' or vice versa, right? Don't you in a relationship? Well, I expect him to talk. If you don't talk, you're being rude, right? Well, that's God. No, it's relationship. He's my friend. I expect him to talk. And if he doesn't talk, I've done something wrong. I've either upset him, just like if I've hurt Diana or I've said something rude, she doesn't talk. I'm like, 'What's wrong? I got to make it right. What did I say? I did something rude.' Does that make sense? Well, it's a relationship. And most people look at it and go, 'Well, it's the thouest. I'm going to take it and I'm going to be all religious today, and I'm going to sit at his feet, and I'm going to move him.' I just look at him and say, 'Hey, Jesus, I'm having a bad day today. Today sucked. I need help.' You know, I try to be as real as I can with the Father. You know, whenever you get real with God, God gets real with you. I say it all the time. That's real. That comes out of me. When you get real with God, God will get real with you. All this religious crap is just crap. People falling morally, people messing up with church finances—all this stuff is crap. Where is he at in any of it? I just go, 'Man, I just want him.' But I look at it and say, 'It's a model. If they've been successful and they have this, maybe I have to do that to try to get it to work.' It's like, maybe if you pull this lever, you've got to believe for so much, you've got to have an employee, you've got to... I don't know. I'm walking this out by faith with y'all. I'm just being real. I ain't done nothing to this. God said to make a mega ministry. He wants me to do all this stuff—have a TV channel one day. All these things he's telling me to do. I go, 'A new Daystar, a new all this stuff?' I'm like, 'What?' Do you know what I'm saying? The things he pulls you to do and he tells you you're going to do, I just go, 'I don't know how to get there other than by trusting him.' So, you're on the journey with me. That's all I really had for y'all today. I just talk off my heart. I'm just real. I can't stand all this fakeness. We've got pastors with these big heads and ministers with these big heads and you can't approach them, you can't talk to them. Well, you know, and I get that. Maybe now because people want to touch you, be weird—it's like, whatever. Maybe that's what they've come into, and that's the kind of standard they had to set up. Maybe that's what I'm learning, is that maybe I need to be more guarded and have more boundaries so that I'm not more approachable than what I am. But I promised myself I wouldn't be like some of these people. And I look at them—well, maybe they are the way they are because there are people that are really weird. So, in all that, I have to say, "Hey, maybe I don't blame them." And the more I'm into this, I go, "Oh, there are some strange folks up in here. I'm not weird like that." I just go, "Hey, let's just shake hands and be cool. Let's not be spiritual weird," you know? I'm going, "You're like on another planet. Are you even for real? Are you from here? Like, hello?" You know, some folks kind of trip you out a little bit. They trip Braden out. They all be trying to touch him and stuff, and he's like, "I want to pray for you," and he's like, "No, don't touch me." In church, they grab a hold of him, and it's just creepy. Just creepy. It's like, "Who are you? I don't know you." So, all right. Well, there you go. I want to just jump right into prayer with y'all. I don't have a lot of time here, so I'm trying to do a video for you today. I'm waiting on the air conditioning guy to come. My air conditioner went out the other day. I just go, "Okay, well, I'm waiting on my air conditioner to be fixed now." So, I'm taking communion with y'all now. And so, we just stand. That's what we do in our household whenever things happen. So we just go, "Okay, God, thank you. Thank you for your body. Thank you, Father, that you were bruised. You were beaten. You took it all on the cross for me. This is part of my covenant. This is what you did for me, Father, so that I don't have to have sickness in my body. From the top of my head to the soles of my feet, I'm wall-to-wall Holy Ghost. And Father, I thank you, Lord, that you took it all from me. You were bruised. You were beaten. You were maimed. You did it all for Brandon. You did it all for Diana, Megan, Paisley, all of us. You became sickness for us. And Father, we lift up this body in the name of Jesus. And we thank you, Lord, for all that you've done and all that you're going to do. And I take it in the name of Jesus. I'm healed." Are you healed? I'm healed. Why? I take this. I'm healed. Why? Because he's flowing through me. There's no sickness in Jesus. There's no sickness in him. He can't give you something he doesn't have. "Well, God gave me this." No, God can't give you something he doesn't have. God can't give you something that he doesn't have. "Well, God tempted me to sin." No, he doesn't tempt you to sin. The Bible says that if we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us of our sins and cleanse us from all unrighteousness. The devil comes to do those things. But God didn't do that. Your flesh—you are enticed by your own lust, your own flesh. You've got to crucify your flesh. God didn't do it. The devil tempts you, and he'll mess with you, but you're the one that chose to do it, right? He can't force you to do it is what I'm trying to say. The devil can't force you to sin; you did it on your own. The Bible says in James 4:7, "Submit yourselves therefore unto God. Resist the devil, and he will flee." So we have the ability to submit ourselves to the Lord. Resist the devil, and he will flee. The Bible said that. So is the Bible a lie? No. That means we just need to be better at submitting and resisting. I'm not going to mess up anymore. And I take this blood right now, and I thank you, Father. I may have messed up today, but this covers it all. This is the mercy seat. People get all hung up on communion and where you got it from, whatever. This is just my heart. I thank you, Father, that you forgive me. Thank you, Lord, that you cleanse me from all unrighteousness. Thank you, Lord, that my name is written in the Lamb's Book of Life today. Thank you, Father, that I'm seated at your throne with you. I do this by faith, Father, as a relationship in Jesus' name. Hallelujah! Aren't you excited? He gave it all for us. He became sickness for us. He delivered us by his blood. Either you believe it or you don't. Either you believe it or you don't. That's where I'm at with it. All that yesterday—the Valley of the Bones and all the things I talked about, man, that was amazing. If you didn't watch that video and you're just watching this one, go back and watch the Valley of the Bones. The presence of the Lord was so strong. He said, "Can these bones live?" And I knew he wasn't talking about real bones. He was talking about spiritually dead people, is what I felt inside. And that was amazing. All the things he showed me, the glory, the glory! So, let's pray today. I'm going to take some time with y'all and just pray and see what the Lord has for us. I might have to end this prayer time abruptly because I have the air conditioning man coming, and I just want to let you all know that. So, I'm going to pray for you as long as I can, and then I might have to just end it, and then you can watch this video. I wanted to make you a video today, but I have a lot going on. So, let's pray, and let's see what the Lord does here for this time that I have. So, Father, I just thank you. I thank you, Father, for all that you've done and all that you're going to do. I thank you for intimacy, Father. I thank you, Father, that you become real to these people today. There's so many people here that have no idea who you truly are. They have a religious mindset, not a relationship. And Father, I thank you that you become that relationship to them, that they allow themselves to be vulnerable and to allow you to be their friend.
